# zoder CLI Surface (Command Tree)
#
# Generated by `scripts/ci-generate-cli-surface.sh` from the live
# Clap command tree (the `--help` output of every subcommand).
# This file is a committed snapshot of that output.
#
# CI fails if `ZODER_CLI_SURFACE.txt` drifts from the actual CLI surface.
# To update: `cargo build --quiet && scripts/ci-generate-cli-surface.sh --update`
#
# Format: sectioned `--help` output for the main command and each subcommand.
# The sections are sorted by subcommand name for deterministic output.
#
zoder: Free-first, cost-aware, codex-compatible coding CLI

Free-first, cost-aware, codex-compatible coding CLI

Usage: zoder [OPTIONS] [PROMPT] [COMMAND]

Commands:
  exec                Non-interactive run for automation/CI (codex-compatible). `-` reads stdin
  tui                 Launch the zerocode terminal UI, wired to the local zeroclaw engine. The matching zerocode + zeroclaw binaries ship alongside zoder; zerocode auto-starts an ephemeral daemon if none is running. Extra args are forwarded to zerocode (e.g. `--config-dir DIR`, `--theme NAME`)
  models              List the model corpus (free routing pool by default)
  update              Check for a newer zoder release and update to it. Without `--check`, re-runs the official installer to self-replace this binary
  route               Show the routing decision for a task without executing it
  consult             Rank models for a human to pick from: health (circuit-breaker) + coding capability + SWE Elo — the same signals the router uses. Use `--json` for machine output
  spend               Spend report (local ledger) by period
  report              Usage + chargeback report from the local ledger for a period (day/week/month/quarter/ytd), bucketed by time + by model, with the free-tier counterfactual + savings. Colorized output
  health              Model health / circuit-breaker status
  finops              FinOps observability rollup over the local ledger (no enforcement). Subcommands: `report | advisor | forecast`. Reads from the same `~/.zoder/ledger.jsonl` and `~/.zoder/pricing.json` as `zoder spend` / `zoder report`. Reports only
  providers           List configured providers
  config              Show or validate configuration + corpus
  refresh             Reconcile the model corpus against the live served-model list
  pricing             Pricing catalog: sync per-token rates from public price lists, or look up a model's rate
  reconcile           Reconcile local ledger spend against a provider's billed-dollar cost API (admin key; OpenAI + Anthropic). Org-level, lagged: a nightly true-up, not real-time metering
  sessions            List saved multi-turn sessions
  review              Run a code review against local git state (codex `/review` equivalent). Emits a structured JSON verdict (verdict/summary/findings/next_steps)
  adversarial-review  Adversarial review: challenge the approach, design, and assumptions. Accepts optional focus text after the flags
  rescue              Delegate an investigation or fix to an agentic, write-capable run (codex `/rescue` equivalent). Honors `-C`, `--agent`, `-m`, `--approve`
  status              Show active and recent background jobs (or one job's full detail)
  result              Show the stored final output for a finished background job
  cancel              Cancel an active background job
  jobs                Manage the background-job directories under the zoder state dir: list what's there, or prune the finished ones to reclaim space. Subcommands: `list`, `prune`
  loop                Autonomous fix loop: author -> validate (build/test) -> adversarial review -> fix, repeating until the check passes and the reviewer raises no blocking findings (or `--max-iters`). The author keeps one engine session for continuity; every turn is cost-tracked. "Grind until green", the codex/claude-code workflow on open-source models
  transfer            Print a resumable engine session id for the current working dir (codex `/transfer` equivalent) so a follow-up can `--session <id>`
  session             Start an interactive agentic session (goose `session` equivalent): launches the zerocode terminal UI wired to the engine
  run                 Headless agentic run (goose `run` equivalent): execute a task to completion without the UI. Honors `-C`, `--agent`, `-m`, `--approve`
  recipe              Saved prompt/agent templates (goose `recipe` equivalent)
  mcp                 List MCP extensions/servers configured in the engine (goose extensions)
  configure           Show/validate/edit configuration (goose `configure` equivalent)
  completions         Generate shell completions (bash|zsh|fish|powershell|elvish)
  gate                Run the CI-parity gate against the current repo (or `--root DIR`). Detects ecosystems (Rust/Node/Python/Go), derives a plan from the repo's marker files + baseline OSS-hygiene defaults, runs it under the managed-tool bundle (cargo-deny, cargo-audit, osv-scanner, gitleaks, cyclonedx, govulncheck, pip-audit), and prints the honest-degradation GateReport. The default mode is `strict` (fail-closed); `--local-iterate` records skips instead of failing (use only for inner-loop speed; never to bypass the gate). Exits 0 only when the gate is Green. See `docs/CI-PARITY-GATE.md`
  help                Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Arguments:
  [PROMPT]
          Prompt for a quick run (codex-style: `zoder "fix this"`)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

zoder adversarial-review:

Adversarial review: challenge the approach, design, and assumptions. Accepts optional focus text after the flags

Usage: zoder adversarial-review [OPTIONS] [FOCUS]...

Arguments:
  [FOCUS]...
          Extra focus for the reviewer (free text)

Options:
      --base <BASE>
          

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --scope <SCOPE>
          Possible values:
          - auto:         Working tree if dirty, else the branch vs its base
          - working-tree: Uncommitted + staged changes only
          - branch:       Committed branch changes vs base
          
          [default: auto]

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --panel <M1,M2,...>
          

      --background
          

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder cancel:

Cancel an active background job

Usage: zoder cancel [OPTIONS] [JOB]

Arguments:
  [JOB]
          Job id (default: most recent running)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder completions:

Generate shell completions (bash|zsh|fish|powershell|elvish)

Usage: zoder completions [OPTIONS] <SHELL>

Arguments:
  <SHELL>
          [possible values: bash, elvish, fish, powershell, zsh]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder config:

Show or validate configuration + corpus

Usage: zoder config [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --validate
          Exit non-zero if the configuration is invalid

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder configure:

Show/validate/edit configuration (goose `configure` equivalent)

Usage: zoder configure [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --edit
          Open the config file in $EDITOR

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --validate
          Exit non-zero if the configuration is invalid

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder consult:

Rank models for a human to pick from: health (circuit-breaker) + coding capability + SWE Elo — the same signals the router uses. Use `--json` for machine output

Usage: zoder consult [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --free-only
          Restrict to free/routable models (routable already implies free; this is an explicit, visible filter)

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --limit <LIMIT>
          Show at most N rows

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder exec:

Non-interactive run for automation/CI (codex-compatible). `-` reads stdin

Usage: zoder exec [OPTIONS] [PROMPT]

Arguments:
  [PROMPT]
          

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder finops:

FinOps observability rollup over the local ledger (no enforcement). Subcommands: `report | advisor | forecast`. Reads from the same `~/.zoder/ledger.jsonl` and `~/.zoder/pricing.json` as `zoder spend` / `zoder report`. Reports only

Usage: zoder finops [OPTIONS] [SUB]

Arguments:
  [SUB]
          report | advisor | forecast
          
          [default: report]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --since <SINCE>
          Only include spend on/after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --until <UNTIL>
          Only include spend on/before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --window-days <WINDOW_DAYS>
          Forecast window in days
          
          [default: 30]

      --json
          Emit machine-readable JSON to stdout

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder gate:

Run the CI-parity gate against the current repo (or `--root DIR`). Detects ecosystems (Rust/Node/Python/Go), derives a plan from the repo's marker files + baseline OSS-hygiene defaults, runs it under the managed-tool bundle (cargo-deny, cargo-audit, osv-scanner, gitleaks, cyclonedx, govulncheck, pip-audit), and prints the honest-degradation GateReport. The default mode is `strict` (fail-closed); `--local-iterate` records skips instead of failing (use only for inner-loop speed; never to bypass the gate). Exits 0 only when the gate is Green. See `docs/CI-PARITY-GATE.md`

Usage: zoder gate [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --root <DIR>
          Repo root to gate (default: current working directory)

      --local-iterate
          Local-iterate mode: missing tools become Skipped (Yellow) so you can iterate fast on a dev box. The gate reports which checks were skipped and what install commands restore the strict posture. Use this only for inner-loop speed; the `--strict` default is the posture that earns the "it passed the gate" claim

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --strict
          Strict mode (fail-closed). This is the DEFAULT; the flag exists so `zoder gate --strict` is a legible escape hatch when `--local-iterate` is wired into a wrapper

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --tools-only
          Print what gate tools are installed vs missing + the install hint for each missing tool, then exit. Does not run any steps. Useful for `zoder gate --tools-only` in CI caches

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --plan-only
          Print the derived plan (ecosystems + framework hints + step list) without running anything. Useful for inspecting what the gate would actually do

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --json
          Emit machine-readable JSON to stdout (the GateReport + tool-probe, serialized). Suppresses the pretty renderer

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder health:

Model health / circuit-breaker status

Usage: zoder health [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --probe
          Actively ping every free model and record the result

      --all
          Probe every configured provider's live model catalog (not just the default provider / free chat candidates). Discovery calls `GET /v1/models` per provider; if that fails, falls back to the provider's declared model. Output is a per-provider report with model/status/latency rows

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --install-daily
          Install the daily `--probe --all` sweep into launchd (macOS) or a systemd user timer (Linux). Idempotent: re-running overwrites the existing job

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --uninstall-daily
          Remove the daily sweep installed by `install-daily`. Idempotent

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder jobs:

Manage the background-job directories under the zoder state dir: list what's there, or prune the finished ones to reclaim space. Subcommands: `list`, `prune`

Usage: zoder jobs [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list   List every job directory under the zoder state dir, newest first. Mirrors `zoder status` but does NOT require a job id; prints id / status / started / age / cwd. `--json` emits the structured form for tools to consume
  prune  Remove FINISHED background-job directories to reclaim disk space. NEVER touches a running job. Honors `--older-than`, `--keep`, and `--dry-run`. Default retention: keep finished jobs from the last 7 days (so a recent failed loop is still recoverable for debugging)
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
