          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder loop:

Autonomous fix loop: author -> validate (build/test) -> adversarial review -> fix, repeating until the check passes and the reviewer raises no blocking findings (or `--max-iters`). The author keeps one engine session for continuity; every turn is cost-tracked. "Grind until green", the codex/claude-code workflow on open-source models

Usage: zoder loop [OPTIONS] [TASK]...

Arguments:
  [TASK]...
          What to fix (free text; bare words are joined). Falls back to `-i FILE`, then stdin. Flags (`--check`, `--reviewer`, `-m`, …) may appear in any position; use `-i FILE` for task text starting with `-`

Options:
  -i, --instructions <FILE>
          Read the task/instructions from a file instead of args

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --max-iters <MAX_ITERS>
          Maximum author/review iterations before giving up
          
          [default: 6]

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --check <CMD>
          Validation command that must exit 0 (e.g. "cargo test -p foo"). Run after each author turn; failing output is fed to the next iteration

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --reviewer <MODEL>
          Reviewer model id (default: the routed/`-m` model). Use a different strong model for a genuine adversarial second opinion

      --base <BASE>
          Base ref for branch-scope diffing

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --scope <SCOPE>
          Diff scope shown to the reviewer: auto | working-tree | branch

          Possible values:
          - auto:         Working tree if dirty, else the branch vs its base
          - working-tree: Uncommitted + staged changes only
          - branch:       Committed branch changes vs base
          
          [default: auto]

      --accept-on-green
          Resolve as soon as `--check` exits 0, treating reviewer findings as advisory (escape hatch for over-strict reviewers). Requires `--check`

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --allow-dangerous-check
          Skip the pre-exec denylist inspection of the `--check` command string (`rm -rf /`, redirects to `/etc/...`, `dd of=/dev/...`, `curl|sh`, …). Default is to refuse to run a `--check` command that matches the denylist — set this flag to allow such commands explicitly. The denylist is best-effort, not a sandbox; see `exec_safety.rs` for the honest scope statement

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --background
          Run detached as a tracked background job

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder mcp:

List MCP extensions/servers configured in the engine (goose extensions)

Usage: zoder mcp [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list  List MCP servers/extensions configured in the engine config
  get   Get a single MCP server's configuration by name
  test  Test if an MCP server is reachable by name
  help  Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder models:

List the model corpus (free routing pool by default)

Usage: zoder models [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --free
          

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --paid
          

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --all
          

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder pricing:

Pricing catalog: sync per-token rates from public price lists, or look up a model's rate

Usage: zoder pricing [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  refresh  Sync the pricing catalog from public price lists (LiteLLM + OpenRouter)
  show     Show the catalog rate for a model (per-token components, USD per Mtok)
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder providers:

List configured providers

Usage: zoder providers [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder recipe:

Saved prompt/agent templates (goose `recipe` equivalent)

Usage: zoder recipe [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list  List saved recipes in ~/.zoder/recipes
  show  Show a recipe's contents
  run   Run a recipe (JSON: {prompt, model?, agent?, cwd?, oneshot?})
  help  Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder reconcile:

Reconcile local ledger spend against a provider's billed-dollar cost API (admin key; OpenAI + Anthropic). Org-level, lagged: a nightly true-up, not real-time metering

Usage: zoder reconcile [OPTIONS] <PROVIDER>

Arguments:
  <PROVIDER>
          Provider to reconcile: openai | anthropic

Options:
      --days <DAYS>
          Trailing window in days
          
          [default: 14]

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder refresh:

Reconcile the model corpus against the live served-model list

Usage: zoder refresh [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder report:

Usage + chargeback report from the local ledger for a period (day/week/month/quarter/ytd), bucketed by time + by model, with the free-tier counterfactual + savings. Colorized output

Usage: zoder report [OPTIONS] [PERIOD]

Arguments:
  [PERIOD]
          Reporting period: day | week | month | quarter | ytd (period-to-date)
          
          [default: month]
          [possible values: day, week, month, quarter, ytd]

Options:
      --days <DAYS>
          Custom trailing window in days (overrides the period argument)

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --top <TOP>
          Max rows in the by-model table (e.g. `--top 100`). 0 = show all
          
          [default: 20]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --vendor <VENDOR>
          Restrict the report to one vendor profile (e.g. `--vendor enterprise`). Reads `~/.zoder/config.<vendor>.toml` and only counts ledger entries whose `provider` id was contributed by that TOML. The totals, counterfactual, and avoided-spend headline are all recomputed over the filtered set so the headline is meaningful. See README §"Config overlays" for how to add a vendor

      --host <HOST>
          Restrict the report to one publisher host (e.g. `--host meta`), counting every provider that served that publisher's models. The host is the segment before `/` in the model id, so it sums enterprise direct + OpenRouter + any other route for the same publisher. Mutually exclusive with `--vendor` (which scopes by who served the call, not who published the model)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder rescue:

Delegate an investigation or fix to an agentic, write-capable run (codex `/rescue` equivalent). Honors `-C`, `--agent`, `-m`, `--approve`

Usage: zoder rescue [OPTIONS] [TASK]...

Arguments:
  [TASK]...
          What to investigate, solve, or continue (free text; bare words are joined). Flags may appear in any position; use stdin/`-` for text starting with `-`

Options:
      --background
          Run detached as a tracked background job

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder result:

Show the stored final output for a finished background job

Usage: zoder result [OPTIONS] [JOB]

Arguments:
  [JOB]
          Job id (default: most recent)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder review:

Run a code review against local git state (codex `/review` equivalent). Emits a structured JSON verdict (verdict/summary/findings/next_steps)

Usage: zoder review [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --base <BASE>
          Base ref for branch review (e.g. `main`); implies branch scope

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --scope <SCOPE>
          Review scope: auto | working-tree | branch

          Possible values:
          - auto:         Working tree if dirty, else the branch vs its base
          - working-tree: Uncommitted + staged changes only
          - branch:       Committed branch changes vs base
          
          [default: auto]

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
