          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --panel <M1,M2,...>
          Run additional reviewer models in parallel (comma-separated ids)

      --background
          Run detached as a tracked background job (see `status`/`result`)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder route:

Show the routing decision for a task without executing it

Usage: zoder route [OPTIONS] [PROMPT]

Arguments:
  [PROMPT]
          

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder session:

Start an interactive agentic session (goose `session` equivalent): launches the zerocode terminal UI wired to the engine

Usage: zoder session [OPTIONS] [ARGS]...

Arguments:
  [ARGS]...
          Forwarded to zerocode (e.g. `--theme NAME`)

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder sessions:

List saved multi-turn sessions

Usage: zoder sessions [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder spend:

Spend report (local ledger) by period

Usage: zoder spend [OPTIONS] [PERIOD]

Arguments:
  [PERIOD]
          day | week | month | year
          
          [default: month]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --since <SINCE>
          Only include spend on/after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --until <UNTIL>
          Only include spend on/before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --by-model
          Group by model instead of by period bucket

      --host <HOST>
          Restrict to one publisher host (the segment before `/` in the model id, e.g. `--host meta`), summed across every provider that served that publisher's models

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder status:

Show active and recent background jobs (or one job's full detail)

Usage: zoder status [OPTIONS] [JOB]

Arguments:
  [JOB]
          Specific job id (default: list all jobs in this repo/session)

Options:
      --all
          Include jobs from all working directories

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder transfer:

Print a resumable engine session id for the current working dir (codex `/transfer` equivalent) so a follow-up can `--session <id>`

Usage: zoder transfer [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder tui:

Launch the zerocode terminal UI, wired to the local zeroclaw engine. The matching zerocode + zeroclaw binaries ship alongside zoder; zerocode auto-starts an ephemeral daemon if none is running. Extra args are forwarded to zerocode (e.g. `--config-dir DIR`, `--theme NAME`)

Usage: zoder tui [OPTIONS] [ARGS]...

Arguments:
  [ARGS]...
          

Options:
  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

zoder update:

Check for a newer zoder release and update to it. Without `--check`, re-runs the official installer to self-replace this binary

Usage: zoder update [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --check
          Only report whether a newer release exists; do not install

  -m, --model <MODEL>
          Force a specific model id (skips routing)

      --tier <TIER>
          Routing tier: fast | strong | auto | single-pass | grind (default auto). Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time so a typo (e.g. `strogn`) can never silently downgrade to `auto` routing. The accepted set mirrors `zoder_core::router::Tier::parse`
          
          [default: auto]
          [possible values: fast, strong, codex, auto, single-pass, singlepass, single, oneshot, grind, loop]

      --allow-paid
          Allow paid models without interactive confirmation (scripts/CI)

      --require-free
          Only ever use free models; never fall back to paid

      --dry-run
          Print the routing decision + cost estimate without calling

      --explain
          Explain why a model was chosen

      --max-tokens <MAX_TOKENS>
          Max output tokens
          
          [default: 1024]

      --no-stream
          Disable streaming output

      --json
          JSON output (codex/claude-compatible)

      --show-reasoning
          Include model reasoning/thinking in the answer (default: off)

      --reasoning <REASONING>
          Reasoning effort to request: minimal | low | medium | high | none (default: the model's own default). `none` asks it to skip thinking. Out-of-set values are rejected at parse time; the documented set is the contract even though per-provider semantics vary (some drop the field)
          
          [possible values: minimal, low, medium, high, none]

      --lenient-telemetry
          Relax the fail-closed free guard when a backend reports no telemetry

  -C, --cd <DIR>
          Working directory for the agentic run (codex `-C`). Default: current dir

      --agent <ALIAS>
          Run as a specific zeroclaw agent alias (see `zoder agents`/zerocode picker). Default: alias derived from the routed/`-m` model

      --oneshot
          Pure single-shot completion (no tools/file edits). The default is the agentic loop (codex `exec` drop-in) when the engine is reachable

      --approve <POLICY>
          Tool-approval policy for the agentic loop. Must be one of: `all` (auto-approve every tool), `allowlist` (auto-approve only the read-only allowlist), `none` (deny every tool). Unknown values are rejected at parse time so a typo never silently downgrades to the default `allowlist`

          Possible values:
          - all:       Auto-approve every tool (fully autonomous)
          - allowlist: Auto-approve only the read-only allowlist; deny the rest
          - none:      Deny every tool (read-only review runs)

      --agent-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for an agentic turn, in seconds (default 900)

      --persist-session
          Persist the engine-side session id between `zoder` invocations so follow-up runs resume the same engine session instead of spinning up a fresh one every time. The id is stored under `~/.zoder/sessions/engine_sessions.json`, keyed per `(engine_kind, canonical-cwd)` so different repos / engines don't cross-talk. Default OFF (every pre-this-flag run created a fresh session; toggling this flag is the only thing that changes behavior).
          
          The driver falls back to creating a fresh session automatically when the engine rejects a resume (e.g. the session was evicted server-side since the last run), so a stale id never hard-fails a run.

      --loop-timeout <SECS>
          Hard wall-clock budget for a single `loop` phase (author, `--check`, review), in seconds (default 900). The watchdog kills the spawned child AND its process group when the budget elapses, so a wedged child can never hang the loop indefinitely. `agent_timeout` controls the engine's internal turn budget and is independent

      --engine <ENGINE>
          Which agentic engine to drive the loop with: zeroclaw | goose (default zeroclaw — current behavior). `goose` spawns a `goose acp` child process and drives standard ACP over its stdio; model selection is via the spawned process's `GOOSE_PROVIDER` / `GOOSE_MODEL` env (see `GOOSE_PROVIDER` to override the default `openai` provider)
          
          [default: zeroclaw]

      --add-dir <PATH>
          Append PATH to the list of writable roots the engine is allowed to write into when `--enforce-writable-roots` is set. cwd is ALWAYS in the list (today's behavior); this only adds extra roots on top. Repeatable: pass `--add-dir` more than once to grant several additional roots

      --enforce-writable-roots
          Turn ON the writable-root containment boundary. With this flag, every write/edit-class tool call from the engine is checked against the resolved writable roots BEFORE the policy decides; any target that resolves outside the roots is DENIED regardless of `--approve`. Without this flag, the boundary is OFF (today's behavior — no new denials)

      --trust-engine
          Trust the engine's tool-shape identification: when set, an unknown engine, an unknown write tool, or a missing/unparseable path on a known write tool does NOT force a fail-closed deny. **Weakens the boundary** — use only for engines whose schema matrix we have validated exhaustively. The default is fail-closed

      --list-schemas
          Print the per-engine write-tool schema matrix (which (engine, tool_name, path_field) tuples are recognized as write-class) and exit. Useful for auditing what `--enforce-writable-roots` will actually check versus what it would deny fail-closed

      --retries <RETRIES>
          Per-model transient-failure retries (timeouts/429/5xx) before fallback
          
          [default: 2]

      --no-fallback
          Disable the cross-model fallback chain (use only the routed primary)

      --session <SESSION>
          Attach to a named multi-turn session (creates it if missing)

      --continue
          Continue the most-recently-updated session

  -q, --quiet
          Suppress the trailing summary/diagnostic line

  -v, --verbose...
          Increase log verbosity (-v debug, -vv trace). Also honors RUST_LOG

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

# End of CLI surface
